verdict.label: RED · Stand down / roll DTE longer
verdict.desc: Over the weekend the U.S. ran its fourth strike in a week against Iran and Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz “closed until further notice,” retaliating against U.S. bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan and Qatar. Oil jumped ~4% (WTI ~$74, Brent ~$79) and equity futures are falling — Nasdaq 100 futures lead the drop ~−0.8% on a chip rout. On top of the war shock, CPI lands tomorrow (Tue 7/14) and PPI Wed 7/15. Two independent GREEN lockouts are live: composite is back in WARM and a major inflation print is inside 24h. Stand down on new credit spreads today, or push DTE out past both the conflict window and the data. Cash is a position.

focus.jbig: Hormuz “closed” · oil spikes · CPI eve · stand down
focus.jtag: Weekend US strikes + Iran shuts Strait of Hormuz — oil jumps, chips rout, CPI tomorrow
focus.jnote: The environment turned back down over the weekend. The U.S. carried out its fourth strike in a week against Iran on Sunday in retaliation for an Iranian attack on a Cyprus-flagged container ship, and Tehran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed “until further notice” — a claim U.S. Central Command disputes, saying traffic is still flowing. Iran retaliated with strikes on U.S. bases in Kuwait and Bahrain (and reportedly Jordan, Qatar and Oman). Oil jumped about 4% (WTI ~$74.36, Brent ~$79.11), chipmakers led futures lower after SK Hynix’s post-IPO unwind, and equity futures are pointing down. This is a live, open-ended conflict — and it lands the night before CPI. Exactly the tape a Sniper does not sell premium into.
focus.notdown: Energy (XLE, XOM, CVX, OXY) and Defense (LMT, RTX, NOC) are the only groups catching a bid — oil supply fear from the Hormuz closure lifts energy, conflict-demand lifts defense. But these are two-sided war trades, not clean Snipers. One de-escalation or ceasefire headline gaps them straight back down, and CPI tomorrow adds a second catalyst. Watch-only, not table-pounders.
focus.notup: Airlines & transports (DAL, UAL, AAL) and fuel-sensitive cyclicals are pressured by the jet-fuel spike, and chipmakers (Micron, SK Hynix, Sandisk, AMD) are routing pre-market. But selling bear calls into an active-war, CPI-eve tape carries violent-squeeze risk on any peace or soft-inflation headline — the disciplined answer is to sell nothing new today.
focus.why: Why RED? Composite is back in WARM (66) and P(≥1% gap) at 44% is well above the 35% trigger — either alone locks out GREEN. On top of that, CPI is tomorrow (7/14) and PPI Wednesday, and the master rules force at least YELLOW with a major print inside 24h. Layer on an active military exchange with a Hormuz-closure oil shock — an unscheduled, open-ended tail you cannot time or size around. Stand down on new credit spreads, or push DTE well past both the conflict and the data. Cash is a position today.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~15 and rising off Friday’s ~15.03 close as war premium returns pre-market
Big events (next 1–3 days) — CPI Tue 7/14, PPI Wed 7/15, big banks 7/14–15; live war headlines
World risk — active U.S.–Iran exchange; Iran declares Hormuz closed, Gulf bases hit
Volatility trend — front-month VIX ticking up faster than 3-month as headlines reset the curve
Commodities — oil supply shock, +4% on the Hormuz closure; the biggest single swing factor

snapshot.delta: Fri close 15.03 · rising on war news
2-mo high area · oil lifts inflation fear

setups.read: The Hormuz-closure oil shock is real news and energy is the day’s clearest bid — but this is a two-sided war trade, not a clean Sniper. The entire move is a conflict premium: one credible de-escalation or ceasefire headline and crude gives back $4–6 in minutes, gapping your bull put the wrong way. CPI tomorrow adds a second catalyst on top. Watch-only.
setups.check_note: On a RED day, don’t open new premium. If you must, ½ size only after it’s clearly above the open & prior close at 9:35, tight stop — and never through CPI.

setups.read: Conflict-demand lifts the defense primes and structurally the story can run. But the same peace-headline risk cuts the other way: a ceasefire announcement gaps this group down hard. Selling a bull put here is a bet the war continues — not a probability edge a Sniper wants to underwrite. Confirm no earnings inside DTE (LMT/RTX report late-Jul).
setups.check_note: Same logic as energy — the bid is a war premium. Stand aside; revisit only once the tape settles and CPI is past.

setups.read: Jet fuel just jumped again with crude, a direct headwind — the fundamental lean is down. But two problems: a peace or soft-CPI headline squeezes shorts violently, and Delta already reported a beat-and-raise Friday, removing the clean bearish catalyst. A bear call into a VIX-rising, CPI-eve tape is exactly the blow-up 4-VAL exists to prevent.
setups.check_note: War squeeze + CPI risk = no. Do not carry an airline bear call into tomorrow’s inflation print.

setups.read: Chipmakers are routing pre-market — SK Hynix U.S. shares are down ~8% unwinding Friday’s IPO pop, and Micron and Sandisk are off ~4%. The momentum is down, but this is a fast, sentiment-driven AI-memory unwind, not a durable news re-rate, and these names have run 200%+ this year — snap-back squeeze risk is severe. TSM reports this week too. No clean Sniper here.
setups.check_note: Volatile momentum unwind, not a durable catalyst. Stand aside — too whippy to sell premium into, especially pre-CPI.

setups.read: No clean fresh post-earnings gap this morning. The season’s first prints (PEP Thu, DAL Fri) are now outside the 24–48h window, and the big banks (JPM, GS, BAC) don’t report until Tue–Wed alongside CPI/PPI. Nothing has reported-and-gapped in the last 24h that qualifies.
setups.check_note: Nothing to validate — no Type A candidate today.

setups.read: Risk-off gap-down morning — the exact tape where breakouts fail. The S&P closed Friday near a weekly high (~7,575) but futures are pointing lower, and no new clean high-volume ATH breakout is holding into a Hormuz-closure, CPI-eve open. Bull-put breakouts need a calm, bid tape; today isn’t it.
setups.check_note: No qualifying breakout holding its pivot — nothing to check.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Headline whipsaw into CPI
scenarios.sub: Choppy risk-off; the tape lurches on every Iran/Hormuz headline while traders de-risk ahead of tomorrow’s CPI

scenarios.label: Bad case — Escalation + hot CPI
scenarios.sub: Hormuz stays shut or a Gulf base is hit hard AND CPI runs hot; oil toward $85–90, equities gap down

scenarios.label: Good case — De-escalation / soft CPI
scenarios.sub: A ceasefire/backchannel headline or a cool inflation print; oil gives back the spike, relief bounce, vol eases

scenarios.label: Tail — Hormuz closure holds / wider war
scenarios.sub: A durable strait shutdown or regional spread; oil spikes hard, a sharp VIX blow-off across risk assets

risks: Strait of Hormuz closure — Iran declared it shut “until further notice” while the U.S. disputes the claim; roughly a fifth of the world’s oil and gas moves through it. Any confirmed shutdown sends crude (and jet fuel) vertical — the week’s biggest tail.
Iran retaliation on U.S. bases — Tehran says it hit sites in Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan and Qatar; any strike on personnel gaps the whole tape instantly. Number-one thing to watch.

CPI · Tue 7/14, PPI · Wed 7/15 — the week’s macro pivot, landing on top of an oil shock. Markets already price at least one 2026 Fed hike; a hot print stacked on crude is the worst-case combo. Fed Chair Warsh also testifies Tue–Wed.
Oil & the 10-year — WTI ~$74 and climbing feeds inflation fear; the 10Y is back near ~4.56%, a two-month-high area. A rate pop compounds pressure on stocks.

Chip-sector unwind — SK Hynix’s post-IPO reversal (~−8% U.S., −15% Seoul) is dragging Micron, Sandisk and AMD lower and leading Nasdaq futures down. TSM earnings this week add single-stock risk to the AI trade.

earnings.intro: Q2 season shifts into gear this week — the big banks (JPM, GS, BAC, Wells, Citi, Morgan Stanley) report ~Tue–Wed 7/14–15, the same days as CPI and PPI, and TSM, Netflix, J&J, GE Aerospace and UnitedHealth follow. With the market on edge from the Hormuz shock, a single-stock earnings gap on top of headline and inflation risk is a triple catalyst — keep every spread clear of its name’s print and of the data.

rows.why: The real season opener — and it lands the same morning as CPI, stacking macro and single-stock risk. Net interest income and loan-loss commentary set the tone for the whole financials group.
rows.angle: Do not hold a bank spread through Tuesday. The CPI/bank overlap is a double catalyst — stay flat into it.

rows.why: The cleanest signal on AI-chip demand this week — lands amid a sharp memory-chip unwind (SK Hynix, Micron). A strong guide could steady the group; a soft one deepens the rout. High single-stock gap risk either way.
rows.angle: No spread on TSM or its suppliers through the print. Watch it for the AI read, don’t underwrite it.

rows.why: The full financials cohort reports into CPI (7/14) and PPI (7/15), and with Warsh testifying the same days, macro and single-stock risk are fully stacked. Any bank spread must expire before its print.
rows.angle: Keep any financials spread to 7 days or fewer so it clears the earnings date — and mind the CPI/PPI overlap.

earnings.outro: Mon 7/13: quiet on the tape, loud on headlines — the setup is all about tomorrow. It gets real fast: big banks and TSM ~Tue–Thu 7/14–16 alongside CPI/PPI and Warsh’s testimony, then Netflix, J&J, GE Aerospace and UnitedHealth — all against a live Iran/Hormuz backdrop.
